text.title: Jira Workflow Management — Agentic Workflows and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-driven Jira workflow configuration and operation.

list.item: workflow-author (sonnet) — generates workflow JSON from process description; statuses, transitions, validators, post-functions.
automation-author (sonnet) — writes Jira Automation rules in YAML from natural-language intent.
jql-builder (haiku) — translates user questions into JQL with validateQuery=strict validation.
migration-mapper (sonnet) — maps source-tool fields and states to Jira target schema.
scheme-doctor (sonnet) — audits issue type, workflow, screen, and permission schemes for drift.
bulk-transition-runner (haiku) — drives bulk JQL-plus-transition with rate-limit safety at max 5 req/s.

example.code: You are workflow-author. Given process {dod_yaml} for issue type {type}, emit Jira Cloud workflow JSON with statuses, transitions, validators (required fields per transition), and post-functions. Constraints: - Maximum 7 statuses - Every transition must have a screen - No global transitions to "Done" Return JSON only.

You are jql-builder. Translate "{question}" into JQL using fields {schema}. Validate via Jira REST /search?validateQuery=strict. If invalid, propose fix. Return { "jql": "...", "explanation": "...", "sample_count": N }. Never invent field names not in {schema}.

antipattern.description: Agents writing field names from training knowledge rather than the live Jira field schema.
antipattern.reason: Jira custom field IDs differ per instance. Cache /rest/api/3/field and provide the schema in the system prompt; reject any output containing field names not in the schema.

antipattern.description: Bulk transitions without rate-limit handling on Jira Cloud.
antipattern.reason: Jira Cloud enforces approximately 50 req/s per site. Unthrottled bulk transitions hit 429s immediately; exponential backoff with Retry-After header is required.

antipattern.description: Applying workflows designed for Jira Cloud to a Data Center or Server instance.
antipattern.reason: Cloud and DC/Server have different automation engines, API surfaces, and permission models. Code working on one frequently fails on the other.

rule.statement: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints are mandatory at: workflow promotion to production, automation rule promotion, schema changes, and bulk transitions affecting more than 100 issues.
rule.rationale: These operations affect all team members simultaneously and are expensive or impossible to reverse.

text.reference: Bulk transition with rate-limit safety and Retry-After handling
